_comment: nfetch's output is a network body, so it golden-conforms only via NET record/replay: tools/nano-conformance.mjs serves the `net[].response` fixture for `net[].url` over the /dev/__net__ bridge instead of a live fetch. The fixture test/fixtures/example.com.http already starts with 'HTTP/1.1 ', so it is replayed verbatim; nfetch strips everything up to the first CRLFCRLF and prints the body, exiting 0. stdoutSha256 is left TBD so capture.yml fills the authoritative golden against the released runtime (publish.yml then enforces it). If you ever re-record the fixture, reset this back to "TBD".
